commands.review_summary:
The PR makes auto-reply preflight compaction continue after structured stale or missing Codex thread-binding failures, teaches Codex conversation recovery to recreate a cleared binding, and adds focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +16, Tests +131. Total +147 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the source-level failure boundary: current main throws on failed preflight compaction, and Codex compaction can return structured stale or missing binding failures. I did not reproduce the full live Telegram group flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair is indicated because there is no discrete code finding; the blocker is live proof or maintainer acceptance of the proof gap.

target.title: fix: recover Codex binding after stale preflight compaction
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #86211.

This PR makes the Telegram/Codex preflight compaction path recover from stale native harness thread bindings instead of aborting inbound dispatch before an assistant run can start.

Changes:
- treats structured stale_thread_binding / missing_thread_binding native harness compaction failures as recoverable during preflight compaction
- keeps non-binding compaction failures fail-closed and visible
- lets Codex binding recovery create a fresh app-server thread when the old binding has already been cleared
- adds focused regression coverage for both the preflight dispatch path and the cleared-binding Codex recovery path

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Inbound Telegram/Codex dispatch no longer aborts when preflight compaction sees a stale or missing Codex app-server thread binding. The recoverable stale-thread result now returns the current session entry so dispatch can continue, and the binding layer creates a fresh app-server thread if the binding file has already been cleared.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at /root/.openclaw/workspace/openclaw-upstream on Linux with Node 22.22.2, branch fix-86211-missing-thread-preflight, commit ff680bb8beb2219d30eb8b841e42573ade14dde1, rebased on current origin/main.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran targeted OpenClaw runtime checks in the local checkout: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts; node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts; focused npx oxlint on the changed core and extension files.

Evidence after fix: Local terminal output showed agent-runner-memory.test.ts passed with 25 tests, conversation-binding.test.ts passed with 13 tests, and focused oxlint exited 0 for the changed core and extension files. The command output was captured after rebasing on origin/main; PR text uses only redacted placeholders such as <codex-thread-id> and no chat IDs.

Observed result after fix: The stale-binding preflight regression no longer throws "Preflight compaction required but failed: thread not found: <codex-thread-id>" for a structured stale_thread_binding result. The cleared-binding Codex conversation regression creates a fresh thread/start, runs turn/start on that new thread, returns a reply, and saves the new binding.

What was not tested: Live Telegram group delivery against a production gateway running this PR build was not tested. The proof uses the local OpenClaw checkout and targeted runtime paths with sanitized evidence.

## Validation

Passed locally:
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts
- npx oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts
- npx oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts

Also ran node scripts/check-changed.mjs. Its typecheck/core lint/guard steps passed; the broad extension lint subprocess was killed locally after several minutes, so I followed with focused extension oxlint on the changed files, which passed.

## Privacy

All issue and PR evidence is sanitized. Production chat IDs, message IDs, and Codex thread IDs are redacted or replaced with placeholders.
